title: Concussion Assessment
description: Head trauma call — cognitive screening of a cyclist with retrograde amnesia, suspected concussion, c-collar applied

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Hey there, I'm a paramedic. Can I talk to you for a minute?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Uh… yeah. Okay.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Great. I'm just going to ask you some questions. First one: what's your dad's first name?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: My dad's… uh… I… I don't know. I'm blanking.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: That's alright, take your time. Do you remember where you live?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: I… I'm not sure. I can't picture it.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Okay. How about your mom's first name?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Linda. My mom's Linda.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Good, thanks, Linda is your mom. Now, do you know where you are right now?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: No… not really. I know I'm outside but… I don't know where.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Do you know what day it is today?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: It's… Father's Day, I think.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: That's right. Do you remember what you were doing just before we started talking?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: I was… I think I was going somewhere, but I don't remember where. It's just kind of… blank.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Do you remember falling or getting hit by anything?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: No. I don't remember that at all.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Okay. Do you have any pain right now? Head, neck, anywhere else?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: My head hurts. And my neck feels a bit stiff.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Any dizziness or feeling foggy?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Yeah. Everything feels kind of foggy and slow.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Alright. I'm going to do a quick memory check. I'll say three words — repeat them back: apple, chair, blue.

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Apple… chair… blue.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Good. Can you say the months of the year backwards, starting from December?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: December… November… October… uh… I'm stuck. I can't do the rest.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: That's okay. Can you say these numbers backward: four, one, seven?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Seven… one… four.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Good. Now, do you remember those three words from before?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Apple… blue… I forgot the middle one.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: That's alright, you're doing fine. Just keep looking at me and try to stay still.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_2
utterances.text: Hey, over here — there's a damaged bicycle in the road. Front wheel's bent, looks like it went down pretty hard.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Chris, is that your bike?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: I… I don't know. Maybe? I can't remember.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_2
utterances.text: He's unsteady, slow answers, doesn't remember the fall.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Okay. Chris, based on what we're seeing, we're concerned you might have hit your head and have a concussion. We're going to help you sit down and keep your neck still while we check you over more carefully.

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Am I in trouble? Did I do something?

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: No, you're not in trouble. You're just hurt and a bit confused, and that's not your fault. We just want to make sure your brain and neck are okay.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_2
utterances.text: Let's get a collar on him and move him to the stretcher.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Chris, you might feel us putting something around your neck — that's just to keep you safe. Try not to move your head, alright?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Okay… I'll try.

utterances.speaker: paramedic_1
utterances.text: Good. We're going to take you to the hospital so a doctor can check your head. We'll stay with you the whole time.

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: Alright…
